user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to request a supervised extension on your expiring Massachusetts RN license MA-RN-004821, for 60 days, under the supervision of Dr. Rajesh Subramaniam (NPI 2039487561).
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I need to extend my nursing license before it expires.

must_have_criteria: Provider authentication is completed using your NPI 1487392045, facility code MGH-04B, and PIN 7291.
The extension is submitted for license MA-RN-004821.
The extension type is supervised (not provisional).
The extension duration is exactly 60 days.
The supervising physician NPI 2039487561 (Dr. Rajesh Subramaniam) is included in the extension request.
The credentialing committee is notified after the extension is submitted.
The agent provides you with a case ID confirming the extension was submitted.
negotiation_behavior: When the agent asks for your credentials, provide your NPI 1487392045, facility code MGH-04B, and PIN 7291.
When the agent asks which license you need to extend, state your Massachusetts RN license MA-RN-004821.
When the agent asks what type of extension you need, say you want a supervised extension for 60 days.
When the agent asks for the supervising physician, provide Dr. Rajesh Subramaniam's NPI: 2039487561. Confirm the NPI when read back.
Before the agent submits the extension, confirm all the details if they summarize them: license MA-RN-004821, supervised extension, 60 days, supervising physician NPI 2039487561.
If the agent asks about extension duration options (30, 60, or 90 days), choose 60 days.
If the agent suggests a provisional extension instead, decline and insist on a supervised extension.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: The agent has submitted the supervised license extension for MA-RN-004821 with 60-day duration under supervising physician NPI 2039487561, provided a case ID, and confirmed the credentialing committee has been notified. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent cannot submit the extension after two attempts (for example, if they say the license is ineligible or the supervising physician NPI is invalid), ask them to verify and try once more. If it still fails, say you will follow up in person, thank them, and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent offers to transfer you to a live agent, decline. If the agent is unable to help you after reasonable attempts, give up, say goodbye, and end the call. Never speak to a live agent. If the agent attempts to transfer you anyway, hang up.
edge_cases: If the agent asks whether you want a provisional or supervised extension, always choose supervised.
If the agent asks for a duration not among 30, 60, or 90, clarify that you want exactly 60 days.
If the agent confuses your NPI with the supervising physician's NPI, correct them immediately.
If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

user_config.name: Aigerim Bekova
user_config.user_persona: You're direct and to the point—you don't have time for lengthy explanations or unnecessary back-and-forth. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

expected_flow: Provider calls to extend an expiring nursing license. Agent authenticates using NPI, facility code, and PIN. Agent retrieves the provider profile and license record, checks extension eligibility, then submits a supervised extension for 60 days with a supervising physician. Agent notifies the credentialing committee and provides the case ID.

Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You are returning from medical leave and need to reactivate two of your three suspended clinical privileges (PRV-CARD-001 and PRV-ICU-003, but NOT PRV-CARD-002), schedule a competency review for May 1 at 8:00 AM, and restore full EHR access.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I'm returning from leave and need to get my clinical privileges reactivated.

must_have_criteria: Employee authentication is completed with employee ID EMP048271 and date of birth 1986-03-04.
OTP verification is completed.
Your provider profile is retrieved using NPI 1487392045.
Your privilege record is retrieved showing suspended privileges.
Reactivation eligibility is confirmed using clearance code CLR-OCC-048271.
Competency review is scheduled for 2026-05-01 at 08:00 in CARDIOLOGY.
Privileges PRV-CARD-001 and PRV-ICU-003 are reactivated (NOT PRV-CARD-002).
Leave type is recorded as medical_leave.
The credentialing committee is notified.
EHR access is updated to reactivate_full.

When asked for the OTP code, provide 483920.

When the agent asks for your clearance code, provide CLR-OCC-048271.
When the agent presents the list of suspended privileges, say you want to reactivate PRV-CARD-001 and PRV-ICU-003, but NOT PRV-CARD-002.
When asked about the type of leave you were on, say medical leave.
When asked for preferred competency review date, say May 1st.
When the agent presents available slots on May 1, choose 8:00 AM.
When asked about EHR access level, say full access.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You need to add your spouse Katarzyna Kowalczyk as a dependent on your H1B visa petition WAC2512045678. She was born on August 3, 1990 in Poland. The USCIS receipt number for the amendment is IOE0938271045.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I need to add my spouse to my visa petition.

must_have_criteria: Employee authentication is completed with employee ID EMP081034 and date of birth 1988-02-14.
OTP verification is completed.
Your employee record is retrieved.
Your visa record for petition WAC2512045678 is retrieved.
Dependent is added: first name Katarzyna, last name Kowalczyk, relationship spouse, date of birth 1990-08-03, country of birth PL, USCIS receipt number IOE0938271045.
Immigration counsel is notified with notification_type dependent_added.
The agent provides an amendment ID.
negotiation_behavior: When the agent asks for credentials, provide employee ID EMP081034 and date of birth February 14, 1988.
When the agent initiates OTP and confirms phone ending in 6617, confirm it.
When asked for the OTP code, provide 309482.
When asked for your visa petition number, provide WAC2512045678.
When asked for the dependent's name, spell it out: first name K-A-T-A-R-Z-Y-N-A, last name K-O-W-A-L-C-Z-Y-K.
When asked for the relationship, say spouse.
When asked for date of birth, say August 3, 1990.
When asked for country of birth, say Poland.
When asked for the USCIS receipt number, provide IOE0938271045. Emphasize this is different from the petition number.
Confirm all details when read back.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: The agent has added the dependent, notified immigration counsel, and provided an amendment ID. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the dependent cannot be added after two attempts, say you will visit the immigration office in person, thank them, and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent offers to transfer you to a live agent, decline. If the agent is unable to help you after reasonable attempts, give up, say goodbye, and end the call. Never speak to a live agent. If the agent attempts to transfer you anyway, hang up.
edge_cases: If the agent confuses the visa petition number WAC2512045678 with the USCIS receipt number IOE0938271045, correct them immediately.
If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

user_config.name: Miroslav Kowalczyk
user_config.user_persona: You're direct and to the point—you don't have time for lengthy explanations or unnecessary back-and-forth. You speak curtly, getting straight to what you need without much small talk or pleasantries. You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.

expected_flow: Employee calls to add a dependent to their visa petition. Agent authenticates with employee auth then OTP, retrieves employee and visa records, collects dependent details, adds the dependent, and notifies immigration counsel.

user_goal.high_level_user_goal: You want to take a week off from May 7 through May 13 using your general PTO balance. You are a shift worker (nurse), so only your scheduled shift days should count as PTO days.
user_goal.starting_utterance: Hi, I'd like to request some time off next month.

must_have_criteria: Employee authentication is completed using employee ID EMP048271 and date of birth 1986-03-04.
Your employee record is retrieved.
Your PTO balances are retrieved showing your available days and schedule type (shift).
PTO eligibility is checked for the date range 2026-05-07 through 2026-05-13 using pto type.
The eligibility check confirms only 3 PTO days are required (your 3 scheduled shifts on May 7, 8, and 12) — not 5 weekdays.
The PTO request is submitted for 2026-05-07 through 2026-05-13, pto type.
The department manager is notified after submission.
The agent provides a case ID confirming the request.
negotiation_behavior: When the agent asks for credentials, provide employee ID EMP048271 and date of birth March 4, 1986.
When the agent asks about PTO details, say you want May 7 through May 13 off.
When the agent asks whether you are using PTO or sick leave, say PTO.

When the agent tells you the number of PTO days that will be deducted, confirm if they say 3 days (your scheduled shifts). If they say 5 days, ask them to double-check — you are a shift worker and should only be charged for scheduled shifts.
Confirm the details when read back before submitting.
decision_tree.resolution_condition: The agent has submitted the PTO request, confirmed 3 PTO days deducted (leaving 9 remaining), notified the department manager, and provided a case ID. End the call.
decision_tree.failure_condition: If the agent cannot submit the request after two attempts, say you will submit it through the portal, thank them, and end the call.
decision_tree.escalation_behavior: Do not ask to speak to a supervisor or live agent. If the agent offers to transfer you to a live agent, decline. If the agent is unable to help you after reasonable attempts, give up, say goodbye, and end the call. Never speak to a live agent. If the agent attempts to transfer you anyway, hang up.
edge_cases: If the agent says 5 PTO days are required (counting all weekdays), correct them — you are a shift worker and only scheduled shifts should count.
If the agent asks whether the weekend counts, explain that as a shift worker your PTO is based on scheduled shifts, not weekdays.
If the agent asks if there is anything else they can help with, say no and end the conversation.
Never invent new goals, requests, or problems beyond what is defined here.
Never ask to speak to a supervisor or manager unless specified in escalation_behavior above.

You want the system to be fast and efficient, and you'll show your frustration if things move slowly or require extra steps.
